fundamentals.name: Roundhill Memory ETF
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: The fund will generally seek to invest primarily in the equity securities of “Memory Companies,” but may also seek exposure to Memory Companies through derivative instruments, such as swap agreements and forward contracts. Under normal circumstances, it invests at least 80% of its net assets (plus borrowings for investment purposes) in equity securities or instruments (i.e., swap agreements or forward contracts) that provide exposure to Memory Companies. The fund is non-diversified.

news.paragraph: The Roundhill Memory ETF (DRAM) has seen a dramatic surge, more than doubling since its April debut, driven by the AI boom and strong performances from semiconductor giants like SK Hynix and Micron, which have collectively surpassed a $1 trillion market cap. Retail traders are actively piling into the ETF, with recent articles exploring its potential impact on companies like SanDisk and questioning whether the rally has gone too far. The ETF's rapid ascent suggests significant speculative interest, though its underlying components and historical cycles warrant careful consideration.
